game_name: Populous II: Trials of the Olympian Gods

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation for all divine powers; manna generation tied to population size and duration
Directional force effects on terrain and units
Fungus uses Conway's Game of Life rules; area denial mechanic
Transform leader into legendary Greek mythology figures; direct combat intervention
Indestructible, indiscriminate forces that signal urgency to complete objective
Addresses losing position recovery; enables temples in enemy territory

typical_relief_triggers: Successful population growth and manna accumulation
Completion of map with experience point rewards
Strategic use of divine powers to turn tide
typical_tension_triggers: Manna meter depletion from power use
Enemy population growth and territory expansion
Triggering of mythological monsters at 75% map control threshold
Inability to recover when opponent gaining advantage
Pressure to complete map before monster destruction spreads
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive escalation: early maps favor exploration and safe power use; mid-game introduces resource management tension through manna depletion cycles; late-game introduces time pressure via indestructible monster spawn mechanic that creates urgency regardless of player performance

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to power categories and basic manna management; safe context allows experimentation with divine interventions
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased territorial competition; resource scarcity becomes meaningful; introduction of fire/wind/plant powers increases strategic options and decision complexity
difficulty_curve.late_game: Monster spawning at 75% control threshold creates time pressure; mythological creatures are indestructible and indiscriminate, forcing mission completion regardless of optimal play; requires careful power sequencing and population management
notable_spikes: Introduction of legendary heroes (mid-game tactical shift)
Monster spawn threshold (sudden urgency and threat escalation)
Late-game opponent temple construction (removes advantage of overwhelming force alone)

notable_level_patterns.description: Exceptional performance allows skipping several maps; unsatisfying performance advances only 1-3 maps. Experience points awarded (1-5) based on victory scale, power economy, and battle duration. Categories can be upgraded with earned points.
Upon either player reaching 75% map control, indestructible mythological monsters spawn and wander indiscriminately, killing both players' forces. Signals that map must be completed immediately.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Late-game maps where control balance tips significantly

notable_level_patterns.description: 29 powers divided into 6 categories (Earth, Water, Wind, Fire, Plants, People). Each power requires manna meter to reach threshold. Larger populations generate manna faster. Repeated devastating power use empties reservoir, creating resource scarcity decisions.
